root_text.an5.55:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an5.55:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyaṁ ubho mātāputtā vassāvāsaṁ upagamiṁsu—
root_text.an5.55:1.10: Te otiṇṇacittā sikkhaṁ apaccakkhāya dubbalyaṁ anāvikatvā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭiseviṁsu.
root_text.an5.55:2.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an5.55:2.2: “idha, bhante, sāvatthiyaṁ ubho mātāputtā vassāvāsaṁ upagamiṁsu—
root_text.an5.55:2.3: bhikkhu ca bhikkhunī ca, te aññamaññassa abhiṇhaṁ dassanakāmā ahesuṁ, mātāpi puttassa abhiṇhaṁ dassanakāmā ahosi, puttopi mātaraṁ abhiṇhaṁ dassanakāmo ahosi.
root_text.an5.55:2.4: Tesaṁ abhiṇhaṁ dassanā saṁsaggo ahosi, saṁsagge sati vissāso ahosi, vissāse sati otāro ahosi, te otiṇṇacittā sikkhaṁ apaccakkhāya dubbalyaṁ anāvikatvā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭiseviṁsū”ti.
root_text.an5.55:3.2: ‘na mātā putte sārajjati, putto vā pana mātarī’ti?
root_text.an5.55:3.3: Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekarūpampi samanupassāmi evaṁ rajanīyaṁ evaṁ kamanīyaṁ evaṁ madanīyaṁ evaṁ bandhanīyaṁ evaṁ mucchanīyaṁ evaṁ antarāyakaraṁ anuttarassa yogakkhemassa adhigamāya yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, itthirūpaṁ.
root_text.an5.55:3.4: Itthirūpe, bhikkhave, sattā rattā giddhā gathitā mucchitā ajjhosannā.
root_text.an5.55:4.1: Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekasaddampi … ekagandhampi … ekarasampi … ekaphoṭṭhabbampi samanupassāmi evaṁ rajanīyaṁ evaṁ kamanīyaṁ evaṁ madanīyaṁ evaṁ bandhanīyaṁ evaṁ mucchanīyaṁ evaṁ antarāyakaraṁ anuttarassa yogakkhemassa adhigamāya yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, itthiphoṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an5.55:4.2: Itthiphoṭṭhabbe, bhikkhave, sattā rattā giddhā gathitā mucchitā ajjhosannā.
root_text.an5.55:5.1: Itthī, bhikkhave, gacchantīpi purisassa cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati;
root_text.an5.55:5.2: ṭhitāpi …pe… nisinnāpi … sayānāpi … hasantīpi … bhaṇantīpi … gāyantīpi … rodantīpi … ugghātitāpi … matāpi purisassa cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.an5.55:5.3: Yañhi taṁ, bhikkhave, sammā vadamāno vadeyya:

translation_text.an5.55:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an5.55:1.2: Now, at that time a mother and son had both entered the rainy season residence at Sāvatthī,
translation_text.an5.55:1.3: as a monk and a nun.
translation_text.an5.55:1.4: They wanted to see each other often.
translation_text.an5.55:1.5: The mother wanted to see her son often,
translation_text.an5.55:1.7: Seeing each other often, they became close.
translation_text.an5.55:1.8: Being so close, they became intimate.
translation_text.an5.55:1.9: And being intimate, lust overcame them.
translation_text.an5.55:1.10: With their minds swamped by lust, without resigning the training and declaring their inability to continue, they had sex.
translation_text.an5.55:2.1: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.an5.55:3.1: “Mendicants, how could that futile man imagine that
translation_text.an5.55:3.2: a mother cannot lust for her son, or that a son cannot lust for his mother?
translation_text.an5.55:3.3: Compared to the sight of a woman, I do not see a single sight that is so arousing, sensuous, intoxicating, captivating, and infatuating, and such an obstacle to reaching the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.an5.55:3.4: Sentient beings are lustful, greedy, tied, infatuated, and attached to the sight of a woman.
translation_text.an5.55:3.5: They sorrow for a long time under the sway of a woman’s sight.
translation_text.an5.55:4.1: Compared to the sound … smell … taste … touch of a woman, I do not see a single touch that is so arousing, sensuous, intoxicating, captivating, and infatuating, and such an obstacle to reaching the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.an5.55:4.2: Sentient beings are lustful, greedy, tied, infatuated, and attached to the touch of a woman.
translation_text.an5.55:4.3: They sorrow for a long time under the sway of a woman’s touch.
translation_text.an5.55:5.1: When a woman walks, she occupies a man’s mind.
translation_text.an5.55:5.2: When a woman stands … sits … lies down … laughs … speaks … sings … cries … is injured, she occupies a man’s mind. Even when a woman is dead, she occupies a man’s mind.
translation_text.an5.55:5.3: For if anyone should be rightly called ‘an all-round snare of Māra’, it’s ladies.
translation_text.an5.55:6.1: You might chat with someone who has knife in hand.
translation_text.an5.55:6.2: You might even chat with a goblin.
translation_text.an5.55:6.3: You might sit close by a viper,
translation_text.an5.55:6.4: whose bite would take your life.
translation_text.an5.55:6.6: one on one with a lady.
translation_text.an5.55:7.2: with a glance and a smile.
translation_text.an5.55:7.5: It’s not good to sit with such a person,
translation_text.an5.55:7.6: even if she’s injured or dead.
translation_text.an5.55:8.1: These five kinds of sensual stimulation
translation_text.an5.55:8.2: are apparent in a woman’s body:
translation_text.an5.55:9.1: Those swept away by the flood of sensual pleasures,
translation_text.an5.55:9.4: time and destination, and life after life.
translation_text.an5.55:10.1: But those who fully understand sensual pleasures
translation_text.an5.55:10.2: live fearing nothing from any quarter.
translation_text.an5.55:10.3: They are those in the world who’ve crossed over,
translation_text.an5.55:10.4: having reached the ending of defilements.”

variant_text.an5.55:3.4: giddhā gathitā → gathitā giddhā (bj); giddhā gadhitā (sya-all, pts1ed, mr) | ajjhosannā → ajjhopannā (bahūsu)
variant_text.an5.55:7.2: sitena ca → mhitena ca (sya-all, km, pts1ed)
